action.reasoning: Observation: the best submitted checkpoint is the 10-model LightGBM raw-return regression ensemble (ic_mean=0.057296); rank objectives and prediction-space rank/z transforms did not improve Pearson IC. Analysis: the primary metric is daily cross-sectional correlation, while the current model is trained on raw next-day returns whose day-level volatility/outliers can dominate squared-error training. A low-risk orthogonal change is to train the same proven LightGBM preset ensemble on cross-sectionally normalized labels (daily z-scored returns), while still evaluating against raw returns and using only data through 2021. Decision: add a target-normalized training script, train a 10-model z-label ensemble, smoke-test locally on the evaluation period, and submit only if the local metric is competitive.
action.command: python train_cs.py --out checkpoints/cs_z_v1 --num_models 10 --target_transform zscore --include_valid_train && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/cs_z_v1 --output local_pred_cs_z_v1.json
